findings.title: Fallback spec generation is undefined for the primary zero-spec case
findings.detail: The design says to fall back to the format of existing `openspec/specs/*/spec.md` files and assumes `openspec new spec` can create the target structure, but the target scenario is precisely repositories with no baseline specs. If OpenSpec CLI does not provide a usable spec template/create path, implementers have no defined output contract, so acceptance criteria 3, 4, and 6 are not reliably satisfiable. Define a canonical fallback spec template/schema that is known to work with downstream `/specflow`, specify a direct file/directory creation path when CLI support is absent, and normalize/deduplicate capability names before writing `openspec/specs/<name>/spec.md`.

findings.title: The `/specflow` handoff checks directories instead of usable spec files
findings.detail: Task 2.1 checks the number of directories under `openspec/specs/`, but the actual failure mode is the absence of valid baseline spec files. A missing `openspec/specs/` directory, empty capability directories, or directories without `spec.md` would incorrectly bypass the handoff and still fail later. Change the condition to require at least one valid `openspec/specs/*/spec.md` file, and treat missing or empty directories the same way for the AskUserQuestion prompt.

findings.title: CLI-first generation path is built around unsupported spec commands
findings.detail: D4 tells implementers to run `openspec new spec "<normalized-name>"` and then use `openspec instructions` for spec generation, but the current CLI does not support `openspec new spec` at all (`openspec new` only supports `change`), and `openspec instructions specs` requires `--change`, so it is not a usable baseline-spec bootstrap flow as written. This leaves acceptance criterion 4 unimplementable from the current design. Replace this with an exact supported CLI strategy for baseline specs (for example using the `specs` template exposed by `openspec templates --json` plus direct file creation), or explicitly define the flow as fallback-only when no baseline-spec creation API exists.

findings.title: Capability selection depends on an undefined AskUserQuestion multi-select mode
findings.detail: D3 and Task 1.4 assume `AskUserQuestion` has a `multiSelect` checkbox mode, but the existing command set in this repo only demonstrates single-choice buttons or free-text prompts. Because selection/addition/removal of capabilities is a core acceptance criterion, the design needs a supported interaction contract instead of inventing an unproven UI primitive. Specify a concrete fallback such as repeated include/exclude prompts, a free-text edit list, or another documented mechanism.

findings.title: No validation step confirms generated baseline specs are usable before handoff
findings.detail: The tasks end after writing `openspec/specs/<name>/spec.md` and reporting success, but there is no post-generation validation of those baseline specs. If the generated structure is even slightly off, the failure will surface later in `/specflow` or `/specflow.design`, which undermines acceptance criterion 6. Add a validation phase after generation using the CLI's spec validation path, surface per-spec errors to the user, and require repair or explicit skip before claiming the bootstrap is complete.
